methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Pre-Seed → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.5x) to $2M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Climate & Energy (found 0 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 21 same-sector peers exist in Climate & Energy, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Climate & Bio-Manufacturing

data.narrative:
Duranium is a pre-seed stage climate and energy company that has raised $2M+ to date. The broader climate and energy sector comprises 1 public comparable (LNZA) and 8 private peers including TAE Technologies, Redwood Materials, Helion, Commonwealth Fusion Systems, and QuantumScape. However, narrower peer-stage filtering within this universe yielded insufficient same-stage or adjacent-stage comparables to support a reliable peer-median valuation approach.

Given data constraints, the Stage-Progression method is the primary applicable valuation framework. Using the $2M total raised as a proxy base value and applying a 2.5x multiplier typical of pre-seed to next-stage progression yields an implied valuation of $5M. This method assumes Duranium's capital efficiency and risk profile align with sector norms at this stage.

The analysis supports a valuation range of $4M to $6M, with a $5M median point. The $1M range reflects asymmetric uncertainty: the low bound ($4M) represents conservative step-up assumptions, while the high bound ($6M) assumes above-median progression potential. Key uncertainties include the absence of direct peer-stage comparables for cross-validation, the adequacy of raised capital as a value proxy in early-stage climate tech, and founder and product differentiation relative to the broader peer set. Additional stage progression or revenue data would strengthen conviction in this range.
